items.title: Always verify normalization
items.avoid: Using raw model logits or unnormalized histogram counts as probabilities without checking they sum or integrate to 1.
items.prefer: Apply softmax for discrete outputs; confirm Σ p(x) = 1 or ∫ f(x) dx = 1 before computing expectations, losses, or tail risks.
items.why: Invalid distributions break cross-entropy, calibration metrics, and any probability-based decision threshold.
items.example: Before reporting class probabilities from a custom head, run `probs.sum()` on a batch and assert it is 1.0 within floating-point tolerance.

items.title: Match the tool to discrete vs continuous
items.avoid: Using `pdf` for discrete labels or `pmf` for continuous latency measurements.
items.prefer: Use PMF and sums for discrete X (labels, counts); use PDF/CDF and integrals or `cdf` differences for continuous X (time, revenue).
items.why: Mixing representations leads to zero-probability errors and wrong interval calculations.
items.example: For API latency SLA P(X > 200 ms), use `1 - norm.cdf(200, loc=mu, scale=sigma)` on a fitted continuous model—not a histogram bin count as a point probability.
